Monday: Margaret opened with "Jalapeño corn dog bites. We're doing it this week and I need to know right..." - questioning whether the dish had real technique behind it. Tuesday: Margaret confirmed a 60-40 cornmeal-to-flour minimum after testing - "Marcus gets it. Fine. Cornmeal-to-flour ratio is 60-40 minimum or the seal breaks down in the oil." Wednesday: Julian presented three options for the bite-through shot. Thursday: Marcus brought finalized copy leading with cornmeal's moisture-seal mechanic. Friday: Margaret opened with "Final review. Shot two locked, copy locked. Someone tell me what isn't right, because something..." - then answered her own challenge. Marcus credited a science-first approach for letting the copy breathe. Julian tied visual restraint to shot two's success. The group agreed that trusting the work - not defending positions - drove the week's progress.

Saturday: This conversation contains no discussion of "Jalapeno Corn Dog Bites" - it is a technical deployment exchange. Devon confirmed verification passed and pushed the site live.
